target.name: Hermeus
target.founder: AJ Piplica, Skyler Shuford, Glenn Case, Mike Smayda

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hermeus, a Series C frontier-tech company developing supersonic and hypersonic aircraft, was last valued at $1.0B post-money after raising $500M to date. The comp universe in Supersonic & Hypersonic is nascent: two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one private peer (Boom Supersonic) are identifiable, though sector depth remains limited.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $1.0B last post-money valuation as a base and applying a standard 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. Peer-median analysis was not feasible given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Supersonic & Hypersonic sector to establish a statistically meaningful median; relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median point estimate of $1.7B. This range reflects typical Series C progression dynamics applied to Hermeus's current post-money valuation. The primary uncertainty stems from the sparse comp set—only one adjacent private peer exists in the sector, limiting external validation of the stage multiplier. Market readiness timelines and regulatory pathways for hypersonic platforms represent additional material valuation risks not captured in this structural analysis.
